Predictive History
Students Volume I path Volumes YouTube Substack About this site

Professor Jiang Xueqin

Predictive History

Formation, legitimacy, and strategic action — taught as one cumulative arc.

Long-form lectures and writing on civilization, power, and how the past can be read toward the future. This page is a gateway to Jiang’s public teaching and to a structured book manuscript built from the same corpus — so students and subscribers can follow the argument in order, not only as a playlist.

Watch on YouTube Read on Substack Open Geo-Strategy #1 (curated text)
Whose work is this? The ideas, voice, and classroom teaching are Jiang Xueqin’s. Canonical public surfaces are the Predictive History channel and the Predictive History newsletter on Substack. This static site and the linked book materials in this repository are curated presentation — editorial structure, chapter outlines, and analysis layers that support reading alongside those sources. They are not a substitute for the lectures themselves, and they are not offered as a separate authorial claim over Jiang’s philosophy.

If you are a student or a serious viewer

The teaching is cumulative. Geo-Strategy, Civilization, Secret History, Game Theory, and Great Books each extend the same backbone in a different register — then interviews and essays widen the frame. Use the volume links below to move in lecture order; each row points at project docs that mirror the manuscript queue.

In the classroom habit

Start where the public series starts, or jump by volume — but expect callbacks.

  • Geo-Strategy #1 (Volume I)
  • Civilization #1 (Volume II)
  • Thesis map (how claims cluster)

From YouTube

Clips and episodes are fragments. The book line exists to state the system and preserve the arc.

  • Full channel
  • How volumes ship (serial, one corpus per volume)

From Substack

Written essays are Volume VII in the book architecture — same title, different medium.

  • Newsletter home
  • Volume VII scope
  • Essay ↔ lecture crosswalk

The multivolume line

One volume per primary corpus. Links go to markdown in this repo (serve the parent folder with a local HTTP server — see README — so relative paths resolve).

Volume I

Geo-Strategy

Geopolitics, prediction, and strategic structure — the anchor run for the first printed volume.

Reading path (20 chapters) Architecture Chapter queue

Volume II

Civilization

Long arc of formation, myth, and institutions — the civilization lecture series.

Scope Queue

Volume III

Secret History

Power, money, and who narrates the past — pedagogical compression with explicit epistemic care.

Scope Queue

Volume IV

Game Theory

Players, rules, incentives — strategic structure made explicit.

Scope Queue

Volume V

Great Books

Canon, consciousness, and liberation rhetoric — close reads in lecture form.

Scope Queue

Volume VI

Interviews

Long-form dialogue on other channels — curated transcripts, host-attributed.

Scope Queue

Volume VII

Essays (Substack)

Written Predictive History — mapped to the same book lattice as the lecture corpora.

Scope Queue

Book layers in this repository

These files are for readers who want the argument in book form: thesis map, preface, outlines, and quote-forward draft chapters (pilot) — always secondary to Jiang’s own voice on video and in the newsletter.

Orient

  • Preface
  • Introduction
  • Thesis map

Volume I pilot chapters

  • ch01 draft (quote-forward)
  • ch02 draft (quote-forward)
  • Pilot note

Quality & boundaries

  • Book quality doctrine
  • Publishing strategy
  • Transcript verification rubric
